sections.title: OT-1 — Inline Transit-Time Ultrasonic Flow Sensor, DN20 (Full BOM)
sections.content:
### B.1 — L4 Subsystems

```
OUTPUT TYPE (L5) — Inline Transit-Time Ultrasonic Flow Sensor, DN20
|
+-- L4.1  Acoustic Sensing Subsystem
|         The piezoelectric transducer pair (A/B) that converts electrical
|         excitation to acoustic pulses and receives the return signal.
|         Delivers the time-of-flight differential that encodes flow velocity.
|
+-- L4.2  Signal Processing & Electronics Subsystem
|         PCB with MCU/ASIC, time-of-flight measurement, analog front end,
|         temperature sensing, power management, and output interface.
|
+-- L4.3  Hydraulic Body Subsystem
|         The metering spool with precision-machined transducer bores
|         and flow-path geometry; the wetted pressure boundary.
|
+-- L4.4  Enclosure & Sealing Subsystem
          Electronics housing, cover, cable entry, and O-ring sealing system
          (IP-rated, environmental protection).
```

---

### B.2 — Full Decomposition (L4 → L0)

#### L4.1 — Acoustic Sensing Subsystem

```
L4.1  Acoustic Sensing Subsystem
│
└─ L3.1.1  Piezoelectric Transducer Assembly (×2, A-path and B-path)
   │
   ├─ L2.1.1.1  Piezo Element (active sensing crystal/ceramic)
   │            [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
   │
   ├─ L2.1.1.2  Acoustic Backing Layer
   │            Epoxy-based damping material behind piezo; suppresses ring-down.
   │            └─ L0  Epoxy resin + tungsten filler (acoustic impedance tuning)
   │
   ├─ L2.1.1.3  Acoustic Matching Layer
   │            Quarter-wavelength layer between piezo and fluid; maximises
   │            energy transfer across piezo–water impedance mismatch.
   │            └─ L0  Filled epoxy or cast polymer (impedance 2–5 MRayl)
   │
   └─ L1.1.1.4  Transducer Housing / Cap
                Hermetic enclosure pressed or threaded into flow body bore.
                [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
```

#### L4.2 — Signal Processing & Electronics Subsystem

```
L4.2  Signal Processing & Electronics Subsystem
│
├─ L3.2.1  Measurement Electronics PCB
│  │
│  ├─ L2.2.1.1  MCU / Ultrasonic ASIC
│  │            Drives transducers, measures ToF differential, computes flow
│  │            and temperature, runs output protocol.
│  │            [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
│  │
│  ├─ L1.2.1.2  Precision Reference Oscillator (TCXO or crystal)
│  │            Timing reference for TDC / ToF resolution.
│  │
│  ├─ L1.2.1.3  Piezo Driver Stage
│  │            High-voltage or current-mode excitation circuit for burst
│  │            generation at 1–2 MHz.
│  │
│  ├─ L1.2.1.4  Analog Front-End ICs (amplifier + comparator)
│  │            Low-noise receive chain; threshold detection or full ADC path.
│  │
│  ├─ L1.2.1.5  Temperature Sensor Element (Pt1000 / NTC)
│  │            On-body temperature measurement for flow compensation.
│  │
│  └─ L0.2.1.6  PCB Substrate
│               [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
│
├─ L3.2.2  Power Management Module
│  ├─ L1  DC-DC converter or LDO (3.3 V / 5 V rail from 5–24 V supply)
│  └─ L1  Decoupling and bulk capacitor array
│
└─ L3.2.3  Output & Communication Interface
           [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
```

#### L4.3 — Hydraulic Body Subsystem

```
L4.3  Hydraulic Body Subsystem
│
├─ L3.3.1  Flow Spool / Metering Tube
│          Precision machined. Contains transducer bores at defined angle,
│          reflector geometry (if Z-path or W-path), and process connection.
│          [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
│          └─ L0  Brass alloy CW617N/CW614N, or SS 316L, or PPS/PVDF resin
│
├─ L2.3.2  Reflector / Flow-Path Insert (Z-path or W-path geometry)
│          Machined flat or angled surfaces directing acoustic beam.
│          L1 — Reflector plate (stainless insert or integral machined surface)
│
└─ L2.3.3  Process Connection Fittings
           [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
```

#### L4.4 — Enclosure & Sealing Subsystem

```
L4.4  Enclosure & Sealing Subsystem
│
├─ L3.4.1  Electronics Housing / Cover
│          [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
│
├─ L3.4.2  Transducer O-Rings (×2, wetted)
│          Seal between transducer housing and flow body bore; pressure boundary.
│          [ALTERNATIVES — see B.3]
│
├─ L2.4.3  Housing Cover Seal (IP65/IP67, non-wetted)
│          Gasket or moulded seal at electronics cover interface.
│
└─ L1.4.4  Cable Gland / Entry Fitting
           PG7 / M12 cable gland; provides strain relief and IP protection.
```

---

### B.3 — Alternatives at Every Level

#### L4.1 · L2.1.1.1 — Piezo Element (active acoustic element)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PZT-5A / PZT-5H polycrystalline ceramic | ~65% | Stable | MEDIUM | APC International; piceramic.com; analyst est. |
| PZT-4 / PZT-8 (hard PZT, power-ultrasound) | ~10% | Stable | MEDIUM | PI Ceramic, APC |
| PMN-PT single crystal | ~10% | Growing | MEDIUM | CTS Corp PMN-PT brochure; pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov |
| BNT-based lead-free ceramic | ~5% | Growing | LOW | Nature Microsystems 2023; analyst est. |
| AlN thin-film PMUT (MEMS) | ~5% | Emerging | LOW | Nature Microsystems & Nanoengineering 2023 |
| PZN-PT / LiNbO₃ single crystal | ~5% | Stable | LOW | americanpiezo.com; analyst est. |

> **Note:** EU RoHS / REACH pressure on lead-containing PZT is the primary driver behind lead-free BNT and AlN share gain. For DN20 hydronic at 1–2 MHz, PZT-5H dominates due to cost and availability.

#### L4.1 · L1.1.1.4 — Transducer Housing / Cap

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Stainless steel 316L (machined) | ~60% | Stable | LOW | Product catalog analysis (Audiowell, BMAG); analyst est. |
| PEEK polymer | ~25% | Growing | LOW | Analyst est. (corrosive media, thermal isolation) |
| Titanium grade 2 | ~10% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (aggressive media, high-temp) |
| Brass (CW614N) | ~5% | Declining | LOW | Analyst est. |

#### L4.2 · L2.2.1.1 — MCU / Ultrasonic ASIC

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Integrated ultrasonic-sensing MCU (e.g., TI MSP430FR6043 USS) | ~45% | Growing | MEDIUM | TI reference design TIDM; DigiKey; EDN flow meter tutorial |
| Discrete MCU + separate TDC IC (e.g., GP22, TDC7200) | ~35% | Stable | MEDIUM | TI TIDM-ULTRASONIC-FLOW-TDC; NXP DRM138 |
| Custom ASIC (Tier-1 OEM proprietary) | ~15% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (E+H, Siemens, Kamstrup) |
| FPGA-based (high-accuracy / research-grade) | ~5% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. |

> **Note:** Integrated USS-MCU dominates in battery-powered heat meters and compact sensors. TI MSP430FR6043 is the reference design IC for the exact Marquardt application class (compact, low-power, DN20 utility).

#### L4.2 · L0.2.1.6 — PCB Substrate

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR4 epoxy glass (standard) | ~80% | Stable | MEDIUM | Industry standard |
| High-Tg FR4 (≥170°C glass transition) | ~15% | Stable | MEDIUM | Required for 90°C ambient variants |
| Ceramic substrate (alumina) | ~5% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (specialist high-T versions) |

#### L4.2 · L3.2.3 — Output & Communication Interface

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 4–20 mA analog output | ~35% | Declining | MEDIUM | Analyst est. (dominant in process / HVAC OEM) |
| Pulse / frequency output | ~25% | Stable | MEDIUM | Analyst est. (heat meter totaliser interface) |
| M-Bus (EN 13757) | ~20% | Growing | MEDIUM | Analyst est. (EU heat metering mandate) |
| HART (over 4–20 mA) | ~10% | Stable | MEDIUM | Analyst est. (process automation) |
| IO-Link | ~5% | Emerging | LOW | Analyst est. (Industry 4.0 OEM integration) |
| RS-485 / Modbus RTU | ~5% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. |

#### L4.3 · L3.3.1 — Flow Spool Material

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Brass CW617N / CW614N (dezincification-resistant) | ~55% | Stable | MEDIUM | Amazon product listings (Audiowell HS0002/HS0003, BMAG); analyst est. |
| Stainless steel 316L | ~30% | Growing | MEDIUM | Analyst est. (drinking water, higher T, glycol) |
| Reinforced PPS or PVDF polymer | ~12% | Growing | LOW | Analyst est. (corrosive, low-cost OEM) |
| Bronze (CuSn) | ~3% | Declining | LOW | Analyst est. |

> **Note:** Brass dominates in the compact heat-meter / hydronic OEM segment (cost, machinability, sufficient rating at ≤90°C, ≤6 bar). SS 316L gains share in glycol > 30% and drinking-water applications.

#### L4.3 · L2.3.3 — Process Connection Type

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| G-thread / BSP (ISO 228) | ~45% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (EU hydronic / heat meter) |
| NPT (ANSI/ASME B1.20.1) | ~25% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (North America) |
| Flanged PN6/PN16 (DN20 flange) | ~15% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (industrial process) |
| Push-fit / compression (e.g., 22 mm Speedfit) | ~10% | Growing | LOW | Analyst est. (HVAC installer market) |
| Weld-end (butt weld) | ~5% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. |

#### L4.4 · L3.4.1 — Electronics Housing Material

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Thermoplastic (ABS / PC / PA66) | ~55% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (cost, moulding, IP54–IP67) |
| Die-cast aluminium | ~30% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (IP67, robust OEM housing) |
| Stainless steel sheet metal | ~15% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (hygienic / food-adjacent) |

#### L4.4 · L3.4.2 — Transducer O-Ring Material (wetted)

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| EPDM | ~50% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (water/HVAC, UV/steam resistance) |
| FKM / Viton | ~35% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (glycol, oils, wide T range) |
| NBR | ~10% | Declining | LOW | Analyst est. (legacy hydronic) |
| PTFE / FFKM | ~5% | Stable | LOW | Analyst est. (high-T, aggressive media) |

> **For Marquardt DN20 (max 90°C, max 50% glycol):** FKM is technically preferred; EPDM is acceptable for pure water; NBR is marginal at 90°C.

---

### B.4 — Component Category Mapping: Ultrasonic Flow Sensor in End-Product BOMs

The Marquardt DN20 sensor is itself an L5 product of NAICS 334513. It also appears as an L-level component inside larger systems. Two positions are commercially significant:

**Position 1: Inside a Compact Heat Meter (L5 end-product of NAICS 334514)**
```
L4  Metering Assembly
 └─ L3  Flow Measurement Module
     └─ L2  Ultrasonic Flow Sensor (= the Marquardt product as a bought-in subassembly)
             Function: measure volumetric flow; deliver pulse or M-Bus output to heat calculator
             Safety-critical: NO (billing accuracy, not life-safety)
             Alternatives at this position:
               - Ultrasonic transit-time inline sensor          ~50%  (Kamstrup, Siemens, Marquardt)
               - Electromagnetic (magmeter) module             ~18%
               - Vortex sensor module                          ~12%
               - Mechanical (turbine/positive displacement)    ~20%  Declining
```

**Position 2: Inside a Hydronic Controller / Heat Interface Unit (HIU)**
```
L4  Hydraulic Control Assembly
 └─ L3  Flow & Energy Sensing Module
     └─ L2  Ultrasonic Flow Sensor (compact inline, DN20)
             Function: energy-billing sub-metering; commissioning flow balance verification
             Safety-critical: NO
             Alternatives at this position:
               - Ultrasonic inline (transit-time)              ~45%  Growing
               - Electromagnetic inline                        ~30%  Stable
               - Paddle-wheel / turbine insert                 ~20%  Declining
               - Differential pressure (orifice plate)        ~5%   Stable
```

**Insight:** At both positions, the Marquardt sensor competes primarily with electromagnetic (magmeter) modules. The magmeter's advantage is no moving parts and insensitivity to gas bubbles; the ultrasonic's advantage is bi-directional measurement, lower pressure drop, and operation with partially deaerated glycol mixes.

---

---

sections.title: Part B — Per-Output-Type BOMs (NAICS 334513)
sections.content:
> OT-1 (Inline Transit-Time Ultrasonic Flow Sensor, DN20) is documented in full above. The following sections provide L4→L0 BOMs for all remaining output types of NAICS 334513. Output types sourced from industry VN analysis and NAICS 334513 product scope.

**Output type index:**
- OT-2: Electromagnetic (Mag) Flowmeter
- OT-3: Coriolis Mass Flowmeter
- OT-4: Vortex Flowmeter
- OT-5: Turbine Flowmeter
- OT-6: Differential Pressure (DP) Flow Transmitter + Primary Element
- OT-7: Thermal Mass Flowmeter (Gas Service)
- OT-8: Pressure Transmitter / Instrument
- OT-9: Temperature Transmitter + RTD / TC Assembly
- OT-10: Level Instrument (Radar / GWR / Ultrasonic / Hydrostatic)
- OT-11: Analytical Instrument (pH / Conductivity / DO)
- OT-12: Control & Recording Instrument (PID Controller / Paperless Recorder)

---

### OT-2 — Electromagnetic (Mag) Flowmeter

> **Typical specs:** DN25–DN1200, conductive liquids (≥5 µS/cm), process and utility service, IP67, flanged or wafer body
> **Market position:** ~26–32% of global flowmeter market (~$2.1B in 2024); dominant in water/wastewater and chemical service
> **Key OEMs:** Endress+Hauser (Promag), Emerson (Rosemount), Siemens (SITRANS FM), ABB (AquaMaster, ProcessMaster), KROHNE (OPTIFLUX)

```
OUTPUT TYPE (L5): Electromagnetic (Mag) Flowmeter — flanged, conductive liquid, process/utility

  L4.1  Electromagnetic Transducer Subsystem
  │     The Faraday-law sensing core. Generates a magnetic field across the bore;
  │     induced EMF across electrodes encodes flow velocity.
  │
  ├─ L3.1.1  Excitation Coil Pair
  │  ├─ L2.1.1.1  Coil Bobbin
  │  │            PA66-GF or PTFE former (no meaningful alternatives at scale)
  │  ├─ L1.1.1.2  Copper Magnet Wire (AWG 28–34, enameled)
  │  │            L0 — Copper rod + polyimide/polyester enamel coating
  │  └─ L2.1.1.3  Coil Core
  │               alternatives: Silicon steel laminations ~65%, Ferrite ~30%,
  │               Air-core (coreless) ~5%  [LOW]
  │               L0 — Silicon steel sheet / ferrite powder + binder
  │
  ├─ L3.1.2  Measurement Electrode Pair (×2, diametrically opposed, wetted)
  │           alternatives: SS 316L ~55%, Hastelloy C-276 ~20%,
  │           Platinum ~10%, Titanium grade 2 ~10%, Tantalum ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — Alloy bar stock per electrode material
  │
  └─ L3.1.3  Grounding Ring / Electrode (×1–2, end-face, wetted)
             alternatives: SS 316L ~65%, Hastelloy C-276 ~25%, Platinum ~10%  [LOW]

  L4.2  Signal Processing & Electronics Subsystem
  │
  ├─ L3.2.1  Measurement PCB
  │  ├─ L2.2.1.1  MCU / Signal Processor
  │  │            alternatives: Dedicated flowmeter ASIC ~60%,
  │  │            MCU + external ADC ~35%, FPGA ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │  │            L0 — Silicon die (CMOS process)
  │  ├─ L1.2.1.2  Instrumentation Amplifier (×2 differential channels)
  │  │            Low-noise, high-CMRR front-end; detects µV-range EMF signal.
  │  └─ L0.2.1.3  PCB Substrate
  │               alternatives: FR4 ~80%, High-Tg FR4 ~15%, Ceramic ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │
  ├─ L3.2.2  Excitation Current Source
  │           Generates alternating DC or low-frequency AC (~6–12.5 Hz) to
  │           the coils. Alternating avoids electrode polarisation.
  │           L2 — H-bridge driver + precision current-sense resistor
  │
  └─ L3.2.3  Output & Communication Interface
             alternatives: 4-20 mA + HART ~40%, PROFIBUS PA/DP ~25%,
             Pulse/frequency ~15%, Foundation Fieldbus ~10%,
             IO-Link ~5%, Modbus RTU ~5%  [MEDIUM]

  L4.3  Flow Tube & Liner Subsystem
  │     The wetted pressure boundary. Must be non-magnetic (no steel),
  │     electrically insulated inside (liner), and sized to bore.
  │
  ├─ L3.3.1  Flow Tube Body
  │           alternatives: Carbon steel + epoxy/zinc coat ~50%,
  │           SS 304/316L ~35%, PP/HDPE polymer body ~10%,
  │           Ductile iron ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — Carbon steel or SS tube blank
  │
  ├─ L3.3.2  Liner (insulating, wetted inner surface)
  │           alternatives: PTFE ~40%, Hard rubber (natural/EPDM) ~30%,
  │           PFA ~15%, Ceramic (Al₂O₃, 99.7%) ~10%,
  │           Polyurethane ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — PTFE resin / rubber compound / alumina powder + binder
  │
  └─ L3.3.3  Process Flanges (×2)
             alternatives: Carbon steel + zinc-coat ~50%, SS 304 ~30%,
             GF-PP/GRP ~15%, Ductile iron ~5%  [LOW]

  L4.4  Transmitter Housing & Connection Subsystem
  │
  ├─ L3.4.1  Transmitter Housing (integral or remote)
  │           alternatives: Die-cast aluminium (IP67) ~60%,
  │           PA66/PP thermoplastic ~30%, SS 316L sheet ~10%  [LOW]
  ├─ L3.4.2  Cable Entry / Conduit
  │           alternatives: M20×1.5 ~50%, NPT ½" ~30%, M25 ~20%  [LOW]
  └─ L3.4.3  Local Display (optional)
             alternatives: 2-line LCD ~60%, LED 4-digit ~25%,
             2.4" color TFT ~15%  [LOW]
```

**Key differentiator vs. OT-1:** The liner replaces all wetted seals/O-rings. Liner choice drives media compatibility, temperature rating, and abrasion resistance. Ceramic liner = no mechanical seal failure mode, but brittle and limited to DN15–DN200. PTFE covers widest chemical range.

---

### OT-3 — Coriolis Mass Flowmeter

> **Typical specs:** DN6–DN150, direct mass flow + density, ±0.1% accuracy, SS or Hastelloy, process/custody-transfer
> **Market position:** Highest ASP of all flowmeter types (~$3,000–$25,000); ~$1.5–1.8B globally (2024); growing at 5% CAGR
> **Key OEMs:** Emerson (Micro Motion ELITE), Endress+Hauser (Promass), KROHNE (Optimass), Yokogawa (RotaMASS)

```
OUTPUT TYPE (L5): Coriolis Mass Flowmeter — dual-tube, flanged, process/custody-transfer

  L4.1  Vibrating Tube Assembly
  │     The primary sensing element. Two matched tubes vibrate at resonance;
  │     Coriolis forces cause phase shift proportional to mass flow rate.
  │     Tube frequency also yields fluid density.
  │
  ├─ L3.1.1  Measuring Tube Pair
  │           alternatives: SS 316L dual U-tube ~40%,
  │           SS 316L dual straight-tube ~30%,
  │           Hastelloy C-276 (U or straight) ~20%,
  │           Titanium grade 9 ~8%, Duplex SS 2205 ~2%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — Seamless SS/Hastelloy/Ti tube (precision wall tolerance ±0.05 mm)
  │
  ├─ L3.1.2  Node Braces / Spacer Bars (×2–4)
  │           Fix tube pair at vibration nodes; define active tube length.
  │           L1 — Machined brace block (same alloy as tubes for thermal expansion match)
  │           L0 — Bar stock (SS 316L / Hastelloy / Ti)
  │
  └─ L3.1.3  Balance Beam / Counterbalance (straight-tube designs only)
             Eliminates vibration transmission to manifold.
             L1 — Counterbalance mass (SS 316L, precision-machined)

  L4.2  Drive & Pickoff Subsystem
  │     Sustains tube resonance and measures the phase/time difference
  │     between inlet and outlet tube motion.
  │
  ├─ L3.2.1  Drive Coil Assembly (×1, midpoint)
  │  ├─ L2.2.1.1  Drive Permanent Magnet (fixed to one tube)
  │  │            alternatives: NdFeB rare-earth ~70%, Alnico ~20%, Ferrite ~10%  [LOW]
  │  │            L0 — NdFeB sintered magnet (Nd₂Fe₁₄B)
  │  └─ L2.2.1.2  Drive Coil (fixed to opposing tube, air gap <1 mm)
  │               L1 — Copper wire coil on SS former
  │               L0 — Enameled copper wire
  │
  └─ L3.2.2  Velocity Pickoff Pair (×2, inlet and outlet)
             alternatives: Electromagnetic (moving-magnet coil) ~90%,
             Optical fiber strain ~5%, Piezoelectric ~5%  [MEDIUM]
             Same coil+magnet construction as drive but in sense-only mode.

  L4.3  Manifold & Case Subsystem
  │
  ├─ L3.3.1  Split Manifold (×2, inlet and outlet)
  │           Distributes flow into tube pair; seals to process connection.
  │           alternatives: Machined SS 316L ~60%, Hastelloy C-276 ~30%,
  │           Titanium ~8%, Duplex SS ~2%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — Bar or forged blank (alloy matches tubes)
  │
  ├─ L3.3.2  Outer Case / Canister
  │           Protects tube assembly; explosion-proof rating possible.
  │           alternatives: SS 304 sheet-metal ~55%, Carbon steel + coat ~35%,
  │           Aluminium alloy ~10%  [LOW]
  │
  └─ L3.3.3  Process Connection Flanges
             alternatives: ANSI 150#/300# (raised face) ~50%,
             EN 1092-1 PN40 ~35%, ASME BPE (sanitary, tri-clamp) ~10%,
             DIN 11851 dairy coupling ~5%  [MEDIUM]

  L4.4  Transmitter Electronics Subsystem
  │
  ├─ L3.4.1  Phase / Frequency Measurement Board
  │           Captures time difference between pickoff signals (µs range);
  │           computes mass flow rate and density.
  │           L2 — FPGA or high-speed DSP + precision time-stamping circuit
  │           alternatives: Proprietary ASIC ~55%, FPGA-based ~35%,
  │           DSP + analog phase detector ~10%  [LOW]
  │
  ├─ L3.4.2  Power Supply & Isolation Module
  │           L1 — DC-DC converter + galvanic isolation transformer
  │
  └─ L3.4.3  Output & Communication Interface
             alternatives: 4-20 mA + HART ~45%, PROFIBUS PA ~25%,
             Foundation Fieldbus ~15%, Modbus RTU ~10%,
             EtherNet/IP ~5%  [MEDIUM]
```

**Key differentiator vs. all other flow types:** Only instrument measuring true mass flow directly (not inferred from volumetric + density). Tube alloy and geometry are the dominant BOM cost drivers (~60% of manufactured cost in the tube assembly alone). No liner, no electrodes, no acoustic elements.

---

### OT-4 — Vortex Flowmeter

> **Typical specs:** DN25–DN300, liquid/gas/steam service, flanged, ±0.5–1% accuracy, up to 400°C
> **Market position:** ~8–12% of global flowmeter market; strong in steam metering
> **Key OEMs:** Endress+Hauser (Prowirl), Emerson (Rosemount 8800), Yokogawa (YEWFLO), Siemens (SITRANS FX)

```
OUTPUT TYPE (L5): Vortex Flowmeter — flanged, liquid/gas/steam, process

  L4.1  Bluff Body & Vortex Sensing Subsystem
  │     The bluff body (shedder bar) creates von Kármán vortex shedding;
  │     the sensor detects pressure oscillations at shedding frequency.
  │
  ├─ L3.1.1  Bluff Body (Shedder Bar)
  │           alternatives: SS 316L ~70%, Hastelloy C-276 ~20%,
  │           Titanium grade 2 ~10%  [MEDIUM]

  │    │           Twin-rod / coaxial ~25%,
  │    │           Rope probe (flexible, SS wire) ~20%,
  │    │           Coaxial (high-viscosity, high accuracy) ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │    │           L0 — Drawn SS 316L or PTFE-coated rod / wire rope
  │    │
  │    └─ L3.1.A3  Probe Process Seal (wetted, top flange)
  │                alternatives: PEEK ~45%, PTFE ~35%, PPS ~20%  [LOW]
  │
  ├─── [Path B — Free-Space FMCW Radar, non-contact]
  │    │
  │    ├─ L3.1.B1  Radar Transceiver / Antenna Module
  │    │           alternatives: 26 GHz band-type FMCW ~55%,
  │    │           80 GHz narrow-beam FMCW ~35%,
  │    │           6.3 GHz pulse radar (legacy) ~10%  [MEDIUM]
  │    │           L0 — SiGe RFIC die + aluminum / plastic horn antenna
  │    │
  │    └─ L3.1.B2  Antenna Type
  │                alternatives: Horn (conical) ~55%, Rod antenna ~25%,
  │                Parabolic dish ~15%, Planar array (80 GHz) ~5%  [LOW]
  │
  └─── [Path C — Hydrostatic Pressure (submersible), low cost]
       │
       ├─ L3.1.C1  Pressure Sensing Capsule
       │           Same as OT-8 L4.1 (piezoresistive dominant ~50%,
       │           capacitive ~40%, strain gauge ~10%)  [MEDIUM]
       │
       └─ L3.1.C2  Submersible Cable / Vent Tube
                   L1 — PUR/PVC cable + capillary vent tube (SS or PTFE)
                   L0 — PUR or PVC compound + SS braid

  L4.2  Signal Processing & Electronics Subsystem
  │     Architecture same across all level technologies.
  │
  ├─ L3.2.1  MCU / Signal Processor
  │           alternatives: ARM Cortex-M + DSP co-processor ~65%,
  │           Proprietary ASIC (VEGA, E+H) ~25%, FPGA ~10%  [LOW]
  │
  └─ L3.2.2  Output & Communication Interface
             alternatives: 4-20 mA + HART ~55%, Foundation Fieldbus ~15%,
             PROFIBUS PA ~15%, Wireless HART ~10%, IO-Link ~5%  [MEDIUM]

  L4.3  Process Connection & Housing Assembly
  │
  ├─ L3.3.1  Process Nozzle Connection
  │           alternatives: Flanged EN/ANSI PN40 / 150# ~50%,
  │           Threaded G1½" ~30%, NPT 1½" ~15%, Tri-clamp (sanitary) ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │
  └─ L3.3.2  Process Wetted Material (connection / probe top fitting)
             alternatives: SS 316L ~65%, Hastelloy C-276 ~20%,
             PVDF ~10%, PTFE-lined ~5%  [LOW]

  L4.4  Transmitter Housing Subsystem
  ├─ L3.4.1  Housing Material
  │           alternatives: Die-cast aluminium ~65%,
  │           PP/PA66 thermoplastic ~25%, SS 316L ~10%  [LOW]
  └─ L3.4.2  Local Display
             alternatives: LCD 4-digit ~50%, 2.4" graphical LCD ~35%,
             Color TFT 3.5" ~15%  [LOW]
```

**Key differentiator:** Highest technology diversity of all NAICS 334513 output types. Radar (GWR + free-space) is displacing all other technologies above DN100/1 m range due to falling RFIC costs. 80 GHz FMCW radar (E+H Micropilot 80) is the current growth driver — tighter beam angle means smaller nozzle, easier installation, and fewer false echoes. Capacitance probes remain dominant in small/simple tanks due to cost.

---

### OT-11 — Analytical Instrument (pH / Conductivity / Dissolved Oxygen)

> **Typical specs:** Inline or submersion probes; pH 0–14, conductivity 0.05 µS/cm–2 S/cm, DO 0–40 mg/L; process or environmental monitoring
> **Market position:** ~$1.8B globally (2024); dominated by water treatment, pharma, food & beverage, and chemical process industries
> **Key OEMs:** Endress+Hauser (Memosens), Mettler-Toledo (InPro), HACH (Orbisphere), Yokogawa (FLXA), Emerson, Hamilton

```
OUTPUT TYPE (L5): Analytical Instrument — inline pH/conductivity/DO transmitter + sensor

  L4.1  Primary Sensing Electrode Assembly
  │     Technology depends on measured parameter (pH, conductivity, or DO).
  │
  ├─ Technology selection (mutually exclusive by parameter):
  │   alternatives (by installed base): pH ~55%, Conductivity ~30%, DO ~15%  [MEDIUM]
  │
  ├─── [Path A — pH Measurement]
  │    │
  │    ├─ L3.1.A1  pH Measurement Electrode
  │    │           alternatives: Glass membrane (H⁺-selective borosilicate) ~65%,
  │    │           ISFET semiconductor (solid-state) ~30%,
  │    │           Metal oxide (e.g., RuO₂, IrOx) ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │    │           L0 (glass): Borosilicate glass bulb (Li₂O-SiO₂-La₂O₃ composition)
  │    │           L0 (ISFET): Si die + Si₃N₄ gate insulator
  │    │
  │    ├─ L3.1.A2  Reference Electrode
  │    │           alternatives: Ag/AgCl single-junction (gel-filled) ~65%,
  │    │           Ag/AgCl double-junction ~25%,
  │    │           Calomel (Hg/Hg₂Cl₂) legacy ~10%  [MEDIUM]
  │    │           L0 — Silver wire + AgCl coating + KCl electrolyte gel
  │    │
  │    └─ L3.1.A3  Reference Electrolyte
  │                alternatives: KCl gel (3.0–3.5 mol/L) ~60%,
  │                KCl liquid ~30%, Proprietary polymer gel ~10%  [LOW]
  │                L0 — KCl salt + gel polymer matrix
  │
  ├─── [Path B — Conductivity Measurement]
  │    │
  │    ├─ L3.1.B1  Conductivity Sensing Element
  │    │           alternatives: Contacting 2-electrode (graphite or Pt) ~40%,
  │    │           Contacting 4-electrode (Pt ring) ~35%,
  │    │           Toroidal/inductive (no contact) ~25%  [MEDIUM]
  │    │           L0 (Pt): Platinum foil / Pt-plated SS
  │    │           L0 (graphite): Vitreous carbon or graphite
  │    │
  │    └─ L3.1.B2  Cell Body Material
  │                alternatives: PEEK ~40%, PVDF ~30%, CPVC ~20%,
  │                Titanium ~10%  [LOW]
  │
  └─── [Path C — Dissolved Oxygen Measurement]
       │
       ├─ L3.1.C1  DO Sensing Principle
       │           alternatives: Amperometric (Clark cell, polarographic) ~50%,
       │           Optical (luminescent O₂-quenching fluorophore) ~40%,
       │           Galvanic (lead/zinc anode) ~10%  [MEDIUM]
       │           L0 (optical): Ru(II)-dye coated sensor cap on glass substrate
       │           L0 (Clark): Pt cathode + Ag anode + KCl electrolyte
       │
       └─ L3.1.C2  Permeable Membrane (Clark/galvanic type)
                   alternatives: PTFE membrane (25 µm) ~55%,
                   Silicone membrane ~35%, FEP membrane ~10%  [LOW]
                   L0 — PTFE/silicone film

  L4.2  Electrode Body & Housing Assembly
  │
  ├─ L3.2.1  Electrode Outer Body Material
  │           alternatives: Glass body (traditional) ~40%,
  │           PEEK/PVDF (robust, digital) ~35%,
  │           SS 316L (fully metallic, Memosens-type) ~25%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — Borosilicate glass tube / PEEK rod / SS 316L tube
  │
  └─ L3.2.2  Process Connection / Installation Type
             alternatives: Submersion (direct immersion) ~40%,
             Inline flow assembly ~35%, Retractable (hot-swap) ~20%,
             Flanged nozzle ~5%  [MEDIUM]

  L4.3  Signal Conditioning Transmitter
  │
  ├─ L3.3.1  Transmitter PCB
  │  ├─ Very high input impedance pre-amp (>10¹² Ω for glass pH electrode)
  │  └─ MCU with temperature compensation (on-board NTC or Pt1000)
  │
  └─ L3.3.2  Output & Communication Interface
             alternatives: 4-20 mA + HART ~55%, Modbus RTU ~20%,
             Digital/inductive (Memosens) ~15%, PROFIBUS ~10%  [MEDIUM]

  L4.4  Process Connection & Enclosure
  ├─ L3.4.1  Probe Wetted Body
  │           alternatives: PEEK ~40%, PVDF ~30%, SS 316L ~20%, CPVC ~10%  [LOW]
  └─ L3.4.2  Transmitter Housing
             alternatives: PP/PA66 compact ~55%, Die-cast Al ~30%, SS ~15%  [LOW]
```

**Key differentiator:** Glass membrane pH electrodes are the most consumed analytical consumable in process industry (replaced every 6–18 months). Memosens (digital inductive coupling, Endress+Hauser) is the dominant design trend — shifts value to the reusable transmitter and standardizes the calibratable-in-the-lab sensor. Optical DO is displacing Clark cell due to zero electrolyte consumption and longer sensor life.

---

### OT-12 — Control & Recording Instrument (PID Controller / Paperless Recorder)

> **Typical specs:** Panel-mount (1/4 DIN, 1/8 DIN) or DIN-rail; 1–64+ channel; PID control or trend recording; -40°C to +50°C ambient
> **Market position:** ~$1.5B globally (2024); panel controllers declining (DCS/PLC displacement); paperless recorders growing in pharma/food compliance roles
> **Key OEMs:** Yokogawa (DX / MW100), Endress+Hauser (RSG), Honeywell (Minitrend GR), JUMO, Eurotherm (by Schneider), Omega Engineering

```
OUTPUT TYPE (L5): Control & Recording Instrument — panel-mount PID controller or paperless recorder

  L4.1  Input / Signal Measurement Subsystem
  │     Accepts process variable signals from sensors and transmitters.
  │
  ├─ L3.1.1  Input Module Type
  │           alternatives: Universal input (TC/RTD/mV/V/mA) ~60%,
  │           Dedicated TC input ~20%, Dedicated RTD ~15%, 4-20 mA only ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │
  ├─ L3.1.2  Analog-to-Digital Converter
  │           alternatives: 24-bit sigma-delta ~55%, 16-bit SAR ADC ~35%,
  │           12-bit legacy ~10%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — Silicon die (CMOS mixed-signal)
  │
  └─ L3.1.3  Input Isolation
             alternatives: Optical isolation (opto-coupler) ~70%,
             Transformer isolation ~20%, No isolation (ground-referenced) ~10%  [LOW]
             L0 — GaAs LED + photodetector pair

  L4.2  Processing & Computing Subsystem
  │
  ├─ L3.2.1  Microcontroller / Processor
  │           alternatives: ARM Cortex-M (32-bit, embedded) ~75%,
  │           Legacy PIC / AVR (8/16-bit) ~20%,
  │           Proprietary DSP ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — Silicon die (CMOS)
  │
  ├─ L3.2.2  Non-Volatile Memory (data storage, recorder function)
  │           alternatives: Flash NAND (internal, 1–16 GB) ~50%,
  │           SD/microSD card ~35%, CF card legacy ~10%, USB flash ~5%  [LOW]
  │           L0 — Silicon die (NAND flash process)
  │
  └─ L3.2.3  Real-Time Clock Module (recorder time-stamping)
             L1 — RTC IC (e.g., DS3231 or equivalent) + coin-cell battery backup
             L0 — CMOS silicon + CR2032 (LiMnO₂)

  L4.3  Display Subsystem
  │
  ├─ L3.3.1  Display Type
  │           alternatives: Color TFT LCD (3.5"–12") ~85%,
  │           Monochrome LCD (2×16 to 4×20 char) ~10%,
  │           LED bargraph + 4-digit numerical ~5%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — TFT glass panel + LED backlight strip
  │
  └─ L3.3.2  Front Panel Interface
             alternatives: Capacitive or resistive touchscreen ~40%,
             Push-button keypad (6-10 keys) ~45%,
             Membrane keypad (flat) ~15%  [LOW]
             L0 — ITO-coated glass (touch) / tactile dome switches (buttons)

  L4.4  Housing, I/O & Connectivity Subsystem
  │
  ├─ L3.4.1  Housing / Mounting Format
  │           alternatives: DIN-rail mount ~50%, 1/4 DIN panel mount ~25%,
  │           1/8 DIN panel mount ~15%, 19" rack-mount ~10%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 — ABS/PC thermoplastic moulding (UL 94 V-0)
  │
  ├─ L3.4.2  Control Output Type (PID controller)
  │           alternatives: Relay output (SPDT) ~50%, 4-20 mA analog ~35%,
  │           Solid-state relay (SSR, triac) ~15%  [MEDIUM]
  │           L0 (relay): Ag alloy contacts + electromagnetic coil + plastic body
  │
  └─ L3.4.3  Communication Interface
             alternatives: RS-485 Modbus RTU ~45%, Ethernet TCP/IP ~30%,
             USB host/device ~15%, Wireless (Wi-Fi/BT) ~10%  [MEDIUM]
             L0 — RJ-45 jack + Ethernet controller IC / RS-485 transceiver
```

**Key differentiator:** The only output type in NAICS 334513 where the primary function is information management rather than physical measurement. PID controller is being displaced by DCS/PLC (boundary with NAICS 334514 and 334112); standalone recorders survive primarily in FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and GAMP 5 regulated environments where instrument-level audit trails are mandatory. Non-volatile data storage (flash vs. SD) is the key reliability differentiator.

---

sections.title: Cross-Output-Type Alternatives Summary
sections.content:
> Components that appear across multiple NAICS 334513 output types — showing how the same BOM position has different dominant alternatives depending on output type.

| Component Position | OT-1 Ultrasonic | OT-2 Mag | OT-3 Coriolis | OT-6 DP | OT-8 Pressure |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Wetted body material** | Brass 55% / SS 30% | CS+coat 50% / SS 35% | SS316L 60% / Hastelloy 30% | SS316L 60% / CS 25% | SS316L 55% / CS 20% |
| **Primary output protocol** | 4-20 mA 35% / Pulse 25% | 4-20mA+HART 40% / PROFIBUS 25% | 4-20mA+HART 45% / ProfibusPA 25% | 4-20mA+HART 55% | 4-20mA+HART 60% |
| **Electronics housing** | Thermoplastic 55% / Al 30% | Die-cast Al 60% / Thermoplastic 30% | SS 316L 55% / Die-cast Al 30% | Die-cast Al 65% / SS 25% | Die-cast Al 60% / SS 25% |
| **PCB substrate** | FR4 80% / High-Tg 15% | FR4 80% / High-Tg 15% | FR4 75% / High-Tg 20% | FR4 80% / High-Tg 20% | FR4 75% / High-Tg 20% |

**Insight:** PCB substrate choice is nearly identical across all output types (FR4 dominates everywhere). The differences that define each output type's BOM are concentrated in L4.1 (the primary sensing element) and L4.3 (the wetted body/liner). Electronics (L4.2) and housing (L4.4) are commoditized across the NAICS.

---
